Relation to UCT: UCT adopts this philosophy at the macroscopic level.
UCT uses macroscopic state representations but does not derive microscopic laws.
UCT applies geometric ideas to constraint-coordinate representations without claiming equivalence.
UCT can provide an effective state representation that may be estimated using state-space methods.
UCT uses physically defined projections rather than purely statistical components.
UCT represents effective couplings among constraint projections.
UCT proposes a continuous state representation that can be compared against boundary-based indicators.
